refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates.' High safety criticality demands explicit audit and change control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and operator identity; (c) all updates are logged with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and persist performance baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, mission context hash).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain and audit-log all performance requirement updates, calibration changes, and baseline revisions.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline → history.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests mission context via API, executes requirement-derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy, availability models), persists baselines to a versioned configuration store, and logs all changes to an immutable audit ledger.
DP-B: Distributed Derivation – Performance requirement logic embedded in Vehicle control software, with baseline storage in local vehicle database and periodic sync to a central audit log.

DP-C: Manual Derivation with Template Library – Site Manager manually selects performance templates based on mission type, system auto-fills from template, and records baseline; changes logged via manual approval workflow.
DP-D: Hybrid Derivation – Mission context ingested by Vehicle, preliminary targets computed locally, sent to central Performance Derivation Service for validation and baseline recording.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with versioned configuration store and immutable audit ledger.

potential_sources: DP-A depends on Telemetry interface to receive mission context from Vehicle and Teleoperation.
DP-A depends on Tasking interface to communicate derived requirements back to Vehicle for operational planning.
DP-A is decoupled from Vehicle control logic; changes to derivation algorithms do not require Vehicle firmware updates.
DP-A is decoupled from Teleoperation UI; derivation logic is independent of operator interface.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling: it is a stateless service that transforms mission context into requirements. Interfaces are well-defined (Telemetry in, Tasking out). Audit ledger is internal and does not couple to external systems. Recommended for high-safety-criticality systems because derivation logic is centralized, testable, and auditable.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) is reliably transmitted via Telemetry interface from Vehicle or Site Manager input.
ASSUMPTION: Performance derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability models) are validated and certified for the worksite domain.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or corrupted, derived requirements may be invalid. Mitigation: validate mission context schema and implement fallback to previous baseline.

RISK: Derivation algorithms may not converge or may produce conflicting targets (e.g., max speed vs. min energy). Mitigation: implement constraint solver with conflict resolution and operator override capability.
RISK: Audit ledger may grow unbounded, impacting performance. Mitigation: implement retention policy and archival strategy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend adding explicit specification of: (1) mission context schema and validation rules, (2) derivation algorithm specifications and certification status, (3) audit ledger retention and archival policy, (4) conflict resolution strategy for competing performance targets.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary. It contains all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and cross-cutting services (Performance Derivation Engine, Configuration Management, Audit Logging). The Performance Derivation Engine is a logical service within NGHE Solution that orchestrates mission context ingestion, requirement derivation, baseline recording, and audit logging.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) flows from Vehicle and Site Manager into Performance Derivation Engine via Telemetry interface.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Derived performance requirements (cycle time targets, energy efficiency targets, availability targets, safety margins) flow from Performance Derivation Engine to Vehicle via Tasking interface for operational planning.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical performance margins and constraint violations are communicated via SafetyAlert interface to Safety Services for real-time monitoring and operator notification.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Performance Derivation Engine communicates with central audit ledger and configuration store via Network Link for baseline persistence and history retrieval.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary and is the natural home for cross-cutting services like Performance Derivation Engine. Containment is high because the engine is a system-level service that orchestrates multiple subsystems. Diffusion risk is minimal (5%) because the engine is logically cohesive and does not require distribution across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services.

Vehicle could host local derivation logic, but this would couple performance requirement derivation to vehicle firmware, making algorithm updates difficult and creating redundancy if multiple vehicles exist. Diffusion risk is high (70%) because derivation logic would be embedded in vehicle control software, complicating testing and audit.

Teleoperation is a user interface and command channel; it is not a suitable home for performance derivation logic. Diffusion risk is very high (80%) because derivation would be mixed with UI concerns, violating separation of concerns.

Safety Services could monitor derived performance targets and enforce safety margins, but the derivation logic itself (algorithms, baseline recording, audit logging) is not a safety service function. Diffusion risk is high (60%) because mixing derivation with safety monitoring would blur responsibilities.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (System Engineering / Performance Management Team)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Derivation Engine is a system-level service that belongs in NGHE Solution. It is logically cohesive (mission context → derivation → baseline → audit), has well-defined external interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking out, SafetyAlert and Network Link for coordination), and does not require distribution across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. Centralizing the engine in NGHE Solution enables algorithm updates, audit compliance, and multi-vehicle scalability.
containment_risks: RISK: If Performance Derivation Engine is not explicitly allocated to a named module within NGHE Solution, it may be orphaned or duplicated across subsystems. Mitigation: create explicit ownership and interface contracts.
RISK: If mission context schema is not standardized, Vehicle and Site Manager may send incompatible data. Mitigation: define and enforce Telemetry interface schema.
RISK: If audit ledger is not replicated or backed up, baseline history may be lost. Mitigation: implement redundant storage and recovery procedures.
RISK: If Network Link is unreliable, baseline recording may fail. Mitigation: implement local caching and eventual consistency.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign Performance Derivation Engine to NGHE Solution with explicit ownership by System Engineering / Performance Management Team. Define detailed interface contracts for Telemetry (mission context schema), Tasking (requirement format), SafetyAlert (margin violations), and Network Link (audit ledger protocol). Specify audit ledger retention policy and archival strategy.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) ingests mission context via Telemetry, (2) computes targets using algorithms, (3) records baseline with metadata, (4) maintains audit log, (5) provides traceability reporting. DP-A is decoupled from Vehicle and Teleoperation, enabling independent evolution. No functional gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (Low): If Performance Derivation Engine is not explicitly named and owned within NGHE Solution, logic may leak into Vehicle firmware or Teleoperation UI. Mitigation: create explicit module boundary and interface contracts.

DIFFUSION RISK (Low): If audit ledger is not centralized, baseline history may be fragmented across Vehicle and central systems. Mitigation: enforce single source of truth for audit log via Network Link.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: DP-A is well-contained in NGHE Solution (containment score 95). The engine is a system-level service with clear external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No significant diffusion into Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services is required. Ownership is clear: System Engineering / Performance Management Team.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) mission context ingestion can be tested via Telemetry interface mock, (2) derivation algorithms can be unit-tested in isolation, (3) baseline recording can be verified via audit ledger inspection, (4) traceability can be validated via end-to-end test from worksite input to history report.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Performance Derivation Engine is allocated to NGHE Solution with explicit ownership. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are named and directed. Sub-FRs are traceable to DP-A components (mission context ingestion, derivation algorithms, baseline store, audit ledger, reporting).

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define mission context schema (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) and validation rules for Telemetry interface.
Specify derivation algorithm specifications (cycle time model, energy efficiency model, availability model) and certification status.
Define audit ledger format, retention policy, and archival strategy.
Create interface control documents (ICDs) for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link.
Allocate sub-FRs to specific software components (e.g., MissionContextValidator, DerivationEngine, BaselineStore, AuditLogger, TraceabilityReporter).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic; it comprises five sub-FRs (mission context ingestion, derivation, baseline recording, audit logging, traceability reporting). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to identify specific algorithms, data structures, and interfaces. DP-A is sound and well-contained in NGHE Solution, but the next level must decompose sub-FRs into atomic requirements and allocate them to specific software components (e.g., MissionContextValidator, DerivationEngine, BaselineStore, AuditLogger, TraceabilityReporter). High safety criticality demands rigorous decomposition and verification at each level.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest and validate worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input via Telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms with conflict resolution.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and persist performance baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, mission context hash) to versioned configuration store.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain and audit-log all performance requirement updates, calibration changes, and baseline revisions with immutable ledger.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline → history.

assumptions: Mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) is reliably transmitted via Telemetry interface from Vehicle or Site Manager input.
Performance derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability models) are validated and certified for the worksite domain.
Network Link is available for baseline persistence and audit ledger access; local caching handles transient outages.
Site Manager and Operator identities are authenticated and logged for audit trail.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are encoded as constraints in the derivation engine.
open_risks: RISK: Mission context may be incomplete or corrupted, leading to invalid derived requirements. Mitigation: validate mission context schema and implement fallback to previous baseline.
RISK: Derivation algorithms may not converge or may produce conflicting targets. Mitigation: implement constraint solver with conflict resolution and operator override capability.
RISK: Audit ledger may grow unbounded, impacting performance. Mitigation: implement retention policy and archival strategy.
RISK: If Performance Derivation Engine is not explicitly owned, logic may leak into Vehicle firmware or Teleoperation UI. Mitigation: create explicit module boundary and interface contracts.
RISK: High safety criticality demands rigorous validation of derivation algorithms and audit trail integrity. Mitigation: implement formal verification and cryptographic audit logging.
evidence_needed: Mission context schema specification and validation rules.
Derivation algorithm specifications (cycle time model, energy efficiency model, availability model) with mathematical formulations.
Certification or validation evidence for derivation algorithms (e.g., test reports, domain expert review).
Audit ledger format specification and retention policy.
Interface control documents (ICDs) for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link.
Software architecture diagram showing Performance Derivation Engine components and interfaces.
Test plan for mission context ingestion, derivation, baseline recording, audit logging, and traceability reporting.
Verification evidence linking sub-FRs to DP-A components and NGHE Solution module boundary.
